description: Added and registered `lowrank_two_stage_residual` in `sparsify/config.py` and `sparsify/sparse_coder.py`. The new class keeps the stable low-rank trunk, splits total K across two sparse residual passes, and reuses a shared decoder for both stages. Exact integration smoke test passed for `ARCHITECTURE=lowrank_two_stage_residual`, `K=32`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` with forward+backward on CUDA. Recommended next run is a controlled full experiment at the established low-K recipe settings.
self_review: This is a clean single-variable architecture addition with whitelist and factory wiring completed. Main residual risk is scientific rather than integration: the 50/50 stage split may not be optimal, but it is the smallest defensible first probe and passed instantiate/forward/backward sanity.

description: Use a parameter-only follow-up on the already integrated `lowrank_two_stage_residual` family. Keep optimizer, LR, EF, and AUXK fixed; change only K from 32 to 24 to test the smaller-K tradeoff lane.
self_review: This respects the one-variable rule and directly targets the stated frontier objective. It avoids additional code churn after the prior family integration and asks the most decision-relevant question next: whether the family still holds up at a meaningfully smaller K.
